prisonSlang.note: Extensive overlap between sources - many duplicate terms with slight definition variations

aaveExtended.note: Many duplicates with existing AAVE database, but includes valuable grammatical concepts

redneckSouthernExpanded.note: Significant overlap with existing Appalachian/Southern database, but includes many new idioms and phrases

criticalObservations.prisonSlangDuplication: Heavy duplication within prison slang sources - same terms appear 3-4 times with identical or near-identical definitions
criticalObservations.aaveGrammarValue: Wolfram PDF provides extensive grammatical concepts that are META-LINGUISTIC and highly valuable for explaining AAVE systematics
criticalObservations.categoryExpansion: This data adds 2 entirely new categories (Prison Slang, Gang Terminology) not previously in database
criticalObservations.sourceQuality: Multiple academic sources (SJSU PDF, Wolfram PDF) provide high-confidence scholarly backing

withinNewData.prisonSlang: Estimate 200+ raw terms → ~100 unique after internal deduplication
withinNewData.aave: Estimate 80+ raw terms → ~40 unique new (many duplicates with existing AAVE database)
withinNewData.redneck: Estimate 100+ raw terms → ~60 unique new (many duplicates with existing Appalachian/Southern)

withExistingDatabase.aaveDuplicates: Expect 30-40 duplicates (finna, slay, hella, bae, woke, cap, slaps, etc.)
withExistingDatabase.redneckDuplicates: Expect 20-30 duplicates (fixin' to, reckon, y'all, bless your heart, etc.)
withExistingDatabase.prisonSlang: Entirely new category - zero duplicates expected with existing database

nextStepsRequired: 1. Deduplicate prison slang internally (Aaron Delgado vs Prison Writers vs Vice)
2. Cross-reference AAVE terms with existing AAVE database (201 terms)
3. Cross-reference Redneck/Southern with existing Appalachian/Southern database
4. Extract all unique terms from each category
5. Build comprehensive master database with ~405 unique terms
dataQualityNote: This dataset is RAW and requires significant deduplication work. Many terms appear 2-4 times across different sources with slight variations in wording. Priority should be given to academic sources (Wolfram, SJSU) over informal sources (Vice, blogs) when definitions conflict.
